subject: [VES spec] publishEventBatch support for stndDefined
commit_message:
[VES spec] publishEventBatch support for stndDefined

publishEventBatch enhanced to support stndDefined events.
All events in batch shall have teh same value of stndDefinedNamespace set.

Change-Id: Ie038c000217d23965742f59e2e33c6e228260202
Signed-off-by: deen1985 <damian.nowak@nokia.com>
Issue-ID: VNFRQTS-935

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Adding code reviewers for stndDefined VNFRQTS change (publishEventBulk operation)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This appears to be a backwards incompatible change.  It doesn't impact the schema, but the spec change could require changes to a client. I don't think this can go in a patch release.  

Given that Guilin and 7.2 is not yet officially published, should we just squeeze this in there and simply head off the issue?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> This appears to be a backwards incompatible change.  It doesn't impact the schema, but the spec change could require changes to a client. I don't think this can go in a patch release.  
> 
> Given that Guilin and 7.2 is not yet officially published, should we just squeeze this in there and simply head off the issue?

Don't see any client impacted here as stndDefined is being introduced under 7.2/Guilin. IMO will be good to get this update for Guilin so new clients will conform to this requirement.

This change is proposed for Honolulu and a post 7.2 version. That is the context for my comment on it being backward incompatible.  If it goes into 7.2, then there is no issue.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Published PS2 here, where I added this to VES 7.2 definition.
I think, we can merge this to master, and they I`ll cherry-pick to Guilin, and we merge it there.

Does this make sense?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Adding additional VES reviewers per https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/VES+Specification+Reviewers+and+Process

All, 

I propose that we go ahead and merge this, and cherry pick back to Guilin prior to the release.  This is a good clarification on how to handle publishEventBatch with stndDefined domain. If we try to push this into a later version of the spec, then we will introduce a backwards incompatible change which could be an impediment to migration to newer versions.

We normally wait a week to merge VES changes, but given the impending Guilin release, I'd like to merge this tomorrow.  If you have an issue, please raise it ASAP.  If you report an issue after tomorrow, then I will do my best to address it before the Guilin release is finalized.

Thanks,
Trevor
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Looks good.
